mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above its 12-month trend, the Fed maintains restrictive monetary policy that suppresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced corporate profit growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during high-rate regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) above 150,000 persons per month signals robust labor market conditions that support consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days without requiring external trigger conditions.
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS=155,350 thousand indicates strong employment
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns during periods of PAYEMS > 150k are not statistically significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1,200 thousand units signals weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand contraction, which reduces construction employment, consumer confidence, and durable goods demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1,200 thousand units for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST=1,309 thousand is above threshold but near boundary
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite HOUST weakness, or correlation between HOUST declines and subsequent SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 3.8%, signaling simultaneous supply weakness and labor market softening that reduces earnings growth expectations and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO=102.95, UNRATE=3.6% shows tight labor market
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns during dual-weakness triggers are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates above 0.8% month-over-month, indicating strong consumer demand that supports corporate revenue growth and profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days in any macroeconomic regime.
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS=697,876 million reflects consumer activity levels
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during periods of strong RSAFS growth are not statistically significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period while payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 50,000 persons per month, signaling labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases > 0.3pp in 3 months AND PAYEMS monthly growth < 50k
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE=3.6% is historically low, PAYEMS=155,350 shows strength
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite labor market deterioration, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real interest rates (approximated by FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL inflation rate) exceed 1.5% in a tightening cycle, compressing equity risk premiums and reducing present values of future corporate cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days when this condition is first triggered.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=4.57% with moderate inflation suggests elevated real rates
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window during high real-rate regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 1.0% month-over-month while real GDP (GDPC1) shows positive quarterly growth, signaling robust supply-side expansion that supports profit growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days in expansion regimes.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 1.0% AND GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO=102.95 reflects recent production levels
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 45% of triggered instances despite supply acceleration, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
